tldr: Switching from react-native-iap to flutter_inapp_purchase implies a full app rewrite from React Native to Flutter — the IAP layer itself is the easy part since both libraries align with the OpenIAP spec.
why_switch: You're already migrating the app from React Native to Flutter and need an equivalent IAP library
Both libraries share the OpenIAP-aligned API surface, easing concept transfer
Type-safe Dart bindings replace TypeScript bindings of similar shape
Continue owning the IAP stack rather than adopting a managed service
Active maintenance on the Flutter library aligns with current StoreKit 2 and Play Billing 8
what_to_export: Product IDs and subscription IDs from App Store Connect and Google Play Console
Receipt validation server endpoints and logic
Purchase event handlers and their side effects
Error code handling for billing edge cases
Localization for paywall and purchase UI strings
Analytics event names tied to purchases
export_method: There is nothing to export from react-native-iap itself — store-side product configuration in App Store Connect and Google Play Console persists across the migration. Document your purchase, restore, and receipt validation logic as a spec so you can re-implement it in Dart. If you have server-side receipt validation, that code is reusable since it runs outside the client.
import_method: Add flutter_inapp_purchase to your pubspec.yaml and rebuild the purchase flow following its OpenIAP-aligned API. Wire it to the same App Store Connect and Google Play product IDs. Reuse your existing receipt validation server. Refer to the flutter_inapp_purchase official docs for the current API surface, since the library tracks OpenIAP changes closely.

concept_mapping.note: Both align with OpenIAP, so concepts map closely — check current docs for exact names
Subscribe to purchase events via the library's stream/callback API
OpenIAP unifies error codes across platforms

effort: Days for the IAP layer alone; weeks-to-months for the full RN-to-Flutter app rewrite
gotchas: This is really an app-framework migration, not just an IAP swap
Test sandbox purchases on both iOS and Android before release
Subscription state continuity for existing users requires careful server-side handling
Native linking may differ; follow the library's installation steps in current docs
Receipt formats and validation are platform-driven, not library-driven, so server logic should still work
Plan a parallel-run period or a staged rollout to catch regressions

email.subject: Note on IAP migration during the React Native to Flutter rewrite
email.body:
Hi team,

For the Flutter rewrite, the IAP layer maps cleanly: react-native-iap and flutter_inapp_purchase both align with the OpenIAP spec, so concepts and product IDs transfer directly. App Store Connect and Google Play config stays as-is, and our receipt validation server is reusable.

The IAP work itself is days, not weeks — the bigger lift is the RN-to-Flutter rewrite. We'll sandbox test on both platforms and stage rollout to protect existing subscribers.

I'll cross-reference the current flutter_inapp_purchase docs before locking exact API calls.
